title: Moniera calycina
intro: Moniera calycina, also known as Indian pennywort, is a creeping herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties, which may help to improve cognitive function and memory. The bioactive constituents of Moniera calycina include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, such as calycin and monierin. In traditional herbal medicine, Moniera calycina is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, and is commonly used in the treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and neurological disorders.
taxonomy: Moniera calycina is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the Himalayas. The plant is a shrub with small white flowers and is commonly found at high altitudes. Moniera calycina is closely related to other members of the Ericaceae family, including Rhododendron and Pieris. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Moniera and the family Ericaceae.
uses: Moniera calycina is known for its traditional medicinal uses in the Western Ghats region of India. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Moniera calycina is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and as a treatment for respiratory problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.

constituents: Moniera calycina contains several active constituents, including iridoid glycosides such as aucubin and catalpol. These compounds have been found to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Additionally, Moniera calycina has been reported to contain other bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids. parts: Moniera calycina. The medicinal parts of Moniera calycina, commonly known as Indian pennywort, include its leaves and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. preparations: Moniera calycina is a plant used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world.
